sections.heading: Why Use an Image Loading Library?
sections.content: Loading images in Android sounds simple but is actually one of the most complex tasks in the platform. Images from the network or disk must be decoded from compressed formats (JPEG, PNG, WebP) into raw Bitmap objects that consume enormous amounts of memory — a single 12MP photo can occupy over 48MB of RAM when decoded. Without a specialized library, developers must manually handle network downloads, memory caching, disk caching, Bitmap pooling, downsampling to fit target view sizes, placeholder display, error handling, and lifecycle cancellation. Image loading libraries like Glide and Coil solve all of these problems out of the box with a single line of code.

sections.heading: What Image Loading Libraries Handle
items: Network downloading — fetches image bytes from URLs on a background thread
Memory caching — keeps recently loaded Bitmaps in an LRU memory cache for instant re-display
Disk caching — persists decoded or original images to disk so network fetches are skipped on subsequent loads
Downsampling — decodes images at the exact size needed by the target ImageView, not full resolution
Bitmap pooling — reuses Bitmap objects from a pool to reduce garbage collection pressure
Placeholder images — shows a loading drawable while the real image downloads
Error images — shows a fallback drawable when the image fails to load
Lifecycle awareness — cancels pending image loads when the associated Activity or Fragment is destroyed
Transformations — applies rounded corners, circular crops, blur, grayscale, and other effects
Animated GIF and WebP support — plays animated formats natively

rows.coil: 100% Kotlin — built from the ground up for Kotlin and coroutines
Native — built on coroutines and OkHttp
Smaller — ~300KB; uses system libraries where possible
Built-in — uses OkHttp by default (shared with Retrofit)

rows.glide: Yes — ties requests to Activity/Fragment lifecycle
rows.coil: Yes — uses Compose lifecycle or coroutine scope

rows.glide: Very mature — used in Google apps; extensive community
rows.coil: Newer but growing rapidly; actively maintained by Instacart

rows.glide: Optional — GlideApp via @GlideModule annotation and kapt
Excellent for large projects and maximum stability
rows.coil: Preferred for new Kotlin-first or Compose projects

sections.heading: Glide — Setup and Basic Usage
sections.content: Glide is developed by Bumptech and used extensively within Google's own apps. It provides a fluent builder API for loading images from URLs, resources, files, and content URIs into ImageViews. Glide automatically manages its own memory and disk caches, handles Bitmap recycling, and ties request lifecycles to Android lifecycle owners. Adding Glide to your project requires a dependency and optionally a GlideModule for custom configuration.

sections.heading: Glide Setup and Installation
items: Add dependency: implementation("com.github.bumptech.glide:glide:4.16.x")
For custom configuration via AppGlideModule, add kapt: kapt("com.github.bumptech.glide:compiler:4.16.x")
For OkHttp integration: implementation("com.github.bumptech.glide:okhttp3-integration:4.16.x")
No additional manifest permissions needed — Glide uses the INTERNET permission already declared for networking
Basic load: Glide.with(context).load(imageUrl).into(imageView)
Glide.with() accepts Context, Activity, Fragment, FragmentActivity, or View
Passing a Fragment or Activity to with() ties the request to that component's lifecycle
Glide.with(applicationContext) loads without lifecycle awareness — use sparingly

rows.description: Sets the image source — String URL, Uri, File, resource ID, or ByteArray
Shows a drawable while the image is loading
Shows a drawable when the image fails to load
Shows a drawable when the load URL is null
Scales and crops the image to fill the ImageView — maintains aspect ratio
Scales image to fit within the ImageView — may leave empty space
Applies a circular crop transformation — commonly used for profile photos
Decodes image at specified dimensions — overrides automatic size detection
Loads a low-quality thumbnail first, then replaces with full-quality image
Controls how the image is cached on disk
Bypasses the memory cache — use for frequently changing images like user avatars
Adds a cross-fade animation when the image replaces the placeholder

sections.heading: Glide Disk Cache Strategies
items: DiskCacheStrategy.ALL — caches both the original source image and the downsampled/transformed result
DiskCacheStrategy.AUTOMATIC — default; Glide decides what to cache based on the source type
DiskCacheStrategy.DATA — caches only the original compressed source data before decoding
DiskCacheStrategy.RESOURCE — caches only the transformed, decoded Bitmap result
DiskCacheStrategy.NONE — disables disk caching entirely — use for real-time or frequently changing images
Clearing disk cache (must be on background thread): Glide.get(context).clearDiskCache()
Clearing memory cache (must be on main thread): Glide.get(context).clearMemory()
Cache size is configurable via a custom AppGlideModule with applyOptions()

rows.description: Scales image to fill view, crops excess — no empty space
Scales image to fit view, may leave empty areas
Clips image into a circle — profile photos
Applies rounded corners to image
Different corner radius per corner
Apply multiple transformations in sequence

sections.heading: Coil — Setup and Basic Usage
sections.content: Coil (Coroutine Image Loader) is a modern image loading library built entirely in Kotlin, using coroutines for async operations and OkHttp for networking. Its API is more idiomatic Kotlin than Glide's Java-heritage API. Coil integrates natively with Jetpack Compose through its AsyncImage composable, making it the recommended choice for Compose-based projects. For traditional View-based apps, Coil provides extension functions on ImageView for a clean, concise loading syntax.

sections.heading: Coil Setup and Installation
items: For View-based apps: implementation("io.coil-kt:coil:2.7.x")
For Compose apps: implementation("io.coil-kt:coil-compose:2.7.x")
For GIF support: implementation("io.coil-kt:coil-gif:2.7.x")
For SVG support: implementation("io.coil-kt:coil-svg:2.7.x")
No annotation processing required — Coil works without kapt or KSP
Coil reuses your app's existing OkHttp instance — no separate HTTP client needed
Basic load (View): imageView.load(imageUrl)
Basic load with config: imageView.load(url) { placeholder(R.drawable.loading); error(R.drawable.error) }
Coil uses a singleton ImageLoader by default — configurable via Coil.setImageLoader()

rows.description: Drawable shown while image loads
Drawable shown when load fails
Drawable shown when URL is null
Enables cross-fade animation between placeholder and loaded image
Enables cross-fade with custom duration in milliseconds
Decodes image at specified size — overrides automatic detection
Applies one or more transformations to the loaded image
Controls memory cache behavior: ENABLED, DISABLED, READ_ONLY, WRITE_ONLY
Controls disk cache behavior: ENABLED, DISABLED, READ_ONLY, WRITE_ONLY

sections.heading: Coil Transformations
items: CircleCropTransformation() — clips image into a circle for avatars and profile photos
RoundedCornersTransformation(radius) — applies uniform rounded corners to all four sides
BlurTransformation(context, radius, sampling) — applies a blur effect to the image
GrayscaleTransformation() — converts the image to grayscale
Apply multiple: imageView.load(url) { transformations(CircleCropTransformation(), BlurTransformation(context, 10f)) }
Transformations are applied in order — the output of each becomes the input of the next
Custom transformations implement the Transformation interface and override transform()
Transformed results are cached with a unique key that includes the transformation parameters

rows.description: Primary Compose composable for loading network images — replaces SubcomposeAsyncImage for most use cases
rows.example: AsyncImage(model = imageUrl, contentDescription = "Profile photo", modifier = Modifier.size(64.dp))

rows.description: Full ImageRequest configuration inside AsyncImage
Compose composable with slots for loading, success, and error states
rows.example: SubcomposeAsyncImage(model = url, loading = { CircularProgressIndicator() }, error = { ErrorIcon() }, contentDescription = null)

rows.description: Returns a Painter for use in Image() composable — for custom image composables

sections.heading: Custom ImageLoader Configuration (Coil)
items: Create a custom ImageLoader: val imageLoader = ImageLoader.Builder(context).okHttpClient(okHttpClient).build()
Share the app's existing OkHttpClient (with auth interceptors) with Coil: .okHttpClient { okHttpClient }
Set global ImageLoader in Application class: Coil.setImageLoader(imageLoader)
Configure memory cache size: .memoryCache { MemoryCache.Builder(context).maxSizePercent(0.25).build() }
Configure disk cache size: .diskCache { DiskCache.Builder().directory(cacheDir).maxSizeBytes(50L * 1024 * 1024).build() }
Add a logger for debugging: .logger(DebugLogger())
Inject the custom ImageLoader via Hilt and pass it to load() calls or AsyncImage
Use a custom ImageLoader when Coil needs to share auth headers or certificates with your Retrofit OkHttpClient

sections.heading: Image Loading in RecyclerView
items: Call Glide.with(fragment) or imageView.load() inside onBindViewHolder() — both libraries cancel previous loads automatically when a View is recycled
Glide handles RecyclerView recycling correctly — starting a new load on a recycled ViewHolder cancels the previous in-flight request
Coil cancels the previous load when a new load() is called on the same ImageView
Set a fixed size on ImageView in your item layout — avoids Glide/Coil needing to calculate size dynamically
Use Glide's RecyclerViewPreloader for pre-loading upcoming items before the user scrolls to them
Use Glide.with(fragment).clear(imageView) to explicitly cancel a load and clear the ImageView
For placeholder stability: set a consistent placeholder so the ImageView doesn't flash blank between recycles
Avoid loading very large images in list items — use thumbnail() in Glide or appropriate size() in Coil

rows.description: Preloads an image URL into Glide's cache without displaying it
Downloads and caches the image file to disk without decoding it
Prefetches an image URL into Coil's disk cache
Automatically preloads upcoming RecyclerView items as the user scrolls
rows.usage: val preloader = RecyclerViewPreloader(Glide.with(this), preloadProvider, sizeProvider, preloadCount); recyclerView.addOnScrollListener(preloader)

sections.heading: Best Practices for Image Loading
items: Always use an image loading library — never manually decode Bitmaps in app code
Set fixed dimensions on ImageViews in XML layouts — prevents the library from needing wrap_content size calculations
Use circleCrop() / CircleCropTransformation() for profile photos instead of ShapeableImageView clipping
Always set a placeholder to avoid the blank flash before images load
Always set an error drawable to handle failed loads gracefully without showing broken image icons
Use the same OkHttpClient instance for both Retrofit and Coil to share connection pools and interceptors
Clear image loads when the View is no longer needed to prevent background work after navigation
Avoid loading full-resolution images into small thumbnails — use override() in Glide or size() in Coil

sections.heading: Common Image Loading Mistakes to Avoid

rows.mistake: Loading images without placeholder or error drawables
rows.fix: Always set .placeholder() and .error() to handle both loading state and failures gracefully
Use Glide.with(fragment) or Glide.with(viewLifecycleOwner) to tie the load to the Fragment lifecycle

rows.mistake: Loading images on the main thread manually
rows.fix: Never use BitmapFactory.decodeStream() on the main thread — always use Glide or Coil for async loading

rows.mistake: Loading full-resolution images into small thumbnail views
rows.fix: Use .override(width, height) in Glide or .size(width, height) in Coil to downsample at load time

rows.mistake: Not clearing loads in RecyclerView onViewRecycled()
rows.fix: Glide and Coil handle cancellation on rebind automatically but explicit Glide.with(ctx).clear(iv) is cleaner

rows.mistake: Disabling disk cache for frequently updated images without reason
rows.fix: For dynamic images, use skipMemoryCache(true) and append a cache-busting query param to the URL instead
Transformations are cached by Glide and Coil after first application — subsequent loads hit the cache

sections.heading: Summary
sections.content: Image loading is one of the most performance-sensitive tasks in Android development, and dedicated libraries like Glide and Coil exist precisely because doing it correctly requires solving a dozen interconnected problems simultaneously. Glide is the battle-tested, feature-rich choice with decades of Android experience behind it. Coil is the modern, Kotlin-first alternative that integrates naturally with coroutines and Jetpack Compose. Both libraries handle caching, downsampling, transformations, placeholders, and lifecycle management automatically. Choose Glide for maximum stability and ecosystem compatibility, Coil for Kotlin-first and Compose projects — and in either case, always set placeholders, use lifecycle-aware contexts, and load images at the appropriate size for their target view.
